system: two coupled ideal spring coordinates with one observed coordinate

practical: encoder or camera tracking x1 while x2 is uninstrumented

regime_assumptions: static quadratic stiffness is the declared object
the visible block H_vv is declared as the ceiling for hidden load
coordinate_note: Both coordinates are displacements in the same units; entries of H can be compared as stiffnesses in this declared coordinate chart.

module_object.description: 2x2 stiffness Hessian with one visible and one hidden coordinate

derivation_status: exact from the stated coupled ideal spring energy

ceiling_or_reference.description: T = H_vv, the stiffness seen before hidden relaxation

positive_scope: This card exposes how an unobserved coupled coordinate renormalises the visible stiffness.
boundary: It does not describe nonlinear springs, dynamic resonance, damping, or active feedback.
failure_mode: The attachment fails if the hidden coordinate is not well approximated by a passive quadratic mode.
next_measurement: Measure x1 stiffness while changing kc or clamping x2 to separate visible block stiffness from relaxed visible stiffness.
